pi_items.source: Task description line 5 in task.json specifies 'Vitamin D3 (Cholecalciferol) 2000 IU capsule'. Verified against medical literature: Vitamin D3 (cholecalciferol) is the standard form of vitamin D supplementation (NIH Office of Dietary Supplements, Healthline, PMC studies on vitamin D supplementation)

Task description specifies 'Dosage: 2000 IU'. Verified against clinical guidelines: 2000 IU daily is a standard and safe dosage within recommended guidelines (800-2000 IU/day) and safe upper limit is 4000 IU/day. Supported by VITAL study showing no toxicity at 2000 IU/day for 5.3 years (News Medical, NIH Office of Dietary Supplements, Frontiers in Nutrition, WebMD)

Task description line 5 specifies 'Reason: Vitamin D deficiency'. Verified as medically appropriate: Vitamin D deficiency is a legitimate clinical indication for supplementation, supported by NIH Office of Dietary Supplements and clinical literature

Found in benchmarks/cua_world/environments/nosh_env/scripts/setup_nosh.sh line 10: ADMIN_USER='admin' and line 166: INSERT ... username='admin' ... for NOSH default admin user. Verified through environment setup script for this NOSH deployment

Found in benchmarks/cua_world/environments/nosh_env/scripts/setup_nosh.sh line 5 (comment), line 11: ADMIN_PASS='Admin1234!', and line 148: password_hash('Admin1234!', PASSWORD_BCRYPT, ...). This is the documented default password for the NOSH ChartingSystem deployment in this environment

privileged_info_summary: NOSH ChartingSystem is a legitimate open-source electronic health record system used in this task environment. The supplement metadata claims are clinically sound: Vitamin D3 (cholecalciferol) 2000 IU daily is an evidence-based dosage for Vitamin D deficiency treatment, within published guidelines (800-2000 IU/day recommended, 4000 IU/day safe upper limit) and supported by clinical trials like VITAL showing safety at this dosage. The default admin credentials (username: 'admin', password: 'Admin1234!') are configured in the environment setup scripts. Patient data is synthetic (generated by Synthea synthetic patient generator), not real patient records.
